concept: Sharp / Having Fine Edge or Point / Keen
description: Having an edge or point able to cut or pierce; keen and precise. 'Tiso' names the quality of sharpness—fine edge, pointed precision.
sound_symbolism: The word cuts clean. 'Ti' begins with precise point, edge defined. Then 'so' completes with clean certainty, sharpness achieved.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'tiso thoe' (sharp blade). Opposite of 'dull' (to be created). Can describe physical edges or mental acuity.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Sharp awareness cuts through confusion—the keen mind perceives clearly.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sharp tools required less force—well-maintained edges made work easier and safer.
